title: About - Grahame Morris MP
headings: h2: Grahame Morris MP Working for Easington
text_excerpt: About - Grahame Morris MP Join Get Involved Donate Grahame Morris MP Working for Easington About Latest News Debates & Questions Tweets@GrahameMorris Contact Twitter Facebook Youtube About Thank you for visiting my website. I hope you find the site useful and always welcome your feedback. As someone born and bred in East Durham, I want to ensure your concerns, issues and questions are heard and represented in parliament. I can do this through tabling Early Day Motions, asking questions, contributing in debates and writing to Ministers, Government Departments and public bodies. I also have a Constituency Team to assist and support constituents with individual issues and enquiries. The casework team have over 50 years of experience and always seek to help constituents: Meet The Team For more information about how we process your data, please see Data Protection Policy and Privacy Notice We always respond to constituent enquiries, however, at times other individuals or organisations may be better placed to assist. Durham County Council County Councillors are your elected representatives who determine the policies and procedures of Durham County Council. If your enquiry relates to a Durham County Council service, the first point of contact should be with your Councillor: Find Your Local County Councillor If you wish to make a formal complaint to Durham County Council please visit: Council Complaints Procedure School Complaints Schools are required to publish their complaints procedure and can be found on the school’s website. They tend to follow a similar process: Stage 1 Informal Stage Complain to staff member Stage 2 Formal Stage Complain to Headteacher Stage 3 Final Stage Complain to Governing Body If you remain dissatisfied with the school’s response, a complaint can be referred to the Secretary of State for Education once you have a final response from the Governing Body Stage 4 Complain to Secretary of State for Education Complainants have a right of appeal to the Secretary of State for Education where (a) A Governing Body is acting or proposing to act unreasonably; or (b) The Governing Body has failed to discharge its duties under the Education Act 1996. The Secretary of State would not act until the school procedures have been completed. An online complaints form is available at gov.uk/complain-about-school Police Complaints Independent Office for Police Conduct: A guide to the police complaints system is available at policeconduct.gov.uk/make-complaint NHS Complaints Information about NHS Complaints system is available at nhs.uk/complaints Other useful links Housing Advice Housing Solutions : durham.gov.uk/article/2892/Housing-Solutions Crisis: crisis.org.uk/get-help/how-to-get-help Shelter: england.shelter.org.uk/housing_advice Benefits Advice East Durham Trust: eastdurhamtrust.org.uk/spied Citizens Advice: citizensadvice.org.uk Welfare Rights: durham.gov.uk/welfarerights Social channels Twitter Facebook Youtube Contact details Constituency Office Seaton Holme Hall Walks Easington County Durham, SR8 3BS Constituency Office Seaton Holme Hall Walks Easington County Durham, SR8 3BS '> View on Google Maps Email: grahame.morris.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Grahame Morris at Constituency Office, Seaton Holme, Hall Walks, Easington, County Durham, SR8 3BS ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
